appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Set the mode and accent colour used across the app. Saved to this device — try it, it changes live.
appearance.languageDescription: Choose the language used across the interface. Saved to your account.

page.title: Settings
page.description: Everything in one place — your account and your workspace. Pick a section to manage it.

members.pending: {count, plural, one {# pending invite} other {# pending invites}}

fields.customFields: {count, plural, one {# custom field} other {# custom fields}}

profile.title: Your profile
profile.description: How you show up across the app. Your avatar and name appear in the header, sidebar, and anywhere your teammates see you.
profile.unsupportedImageDesc: Use PNG, JPG, WebP, or GIF.
profile.photoHint: PNG, JPG, WebP, or GIF. Up to 2 MB.
profile.emailPending: Check the inbox for <old></old> and <new></new> — both need to confirm before the change takes effect.
profile.savedCheckEmail: Profile saved — check your email to confirm the address change

security.title: Login & security
security.description: Change your password and sign out of your devices. These keep your account safe.

password.title: Password
password.description: Use at least {min} characters. You will stay signed in on this device after changing it.
password.noEmail: Cannot change password without a current email
password.tooShort: Password must be at least {min} characters
password.mismatch: New password and confirmation do not match

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.description: Sign out of every device where you're logged in — including this one. Useful if you lost a laptop or shared your password.
sessions.dialogDesc: Every device logged into this account will be signed out and will need to log in again. You will be redirected to the login page.

members.title: Team members
members.description: People with access to this account. Roles control what each teammate can do.
members.expiresInDays: {count, plural, one {expires in # day} other {expires in # days}}
members.expiresInHours: {count, plural, one {expires in # hour} other {expires in # hours}}
members.noResendNote: The plaintext invite URL is only shown once at creation for security — to re-share, revoke the invite below and create a new one.
members.emptyHint: Click <action>Invite member</action> above to generate a shareable link.
members.removeDesc: Remove <name></name> from the account? They'll be signed out of this account and given a fresh personal account on their next sign-in. Their login isn't deleted.

roleDesc.admin: Can invite teammates, manage settings, send messages, and edit data.
roleDesc.agent: Can use the inbox, contacts, broadcasts, automations, and flows. No settings or member access.
roleDesc.viewer: Read-only access across every page. Cannot send or edit anything.

invite.maxLabel: Label must be {max} characters or fewer
invite.serverRetry: Could not reach the server. Try again?
invite.copyBlocked: Clipboard blocked — copy the link manually
invite.waMessage: Join {account} on wacrm using this link (valid for {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}): {url}
invite.createdDesc: Share this link with your new teammate. They'll be able to sign up (or sign in) and join the account as <role></role>. The link is valid for <days></days>.
invite.saveNow: Save this link now. We never store the plaintext — once you close this dialog the URL is gone. To re-share, revoke this invite and create a new one.
invite.formDesc: Generate a one-time invite link. Share it via WhatsApp, Slack, or any channel you like — no email service required.
invite.labelHint: Helps you remember who you sent the link to in the pending list below.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.description: Keys authenticate the public REST API (<path></path>) so you can build your own automations. Send them as <auth></auth>.
apiKeys.emptyHintAdmin: Click <action>New API key</action> to create one.
apiKeys.emptyHintNonAdmin: Ask an admin to create one.
apiKeys.copyDesc: This is the only time the full key is shown. Store it somewhere safe — if you lose it, revoke it and create a new one.
apiKeys.createDesc: Name it after the integration that will use it, and grant only the scopes it needs.
apiKeys.scopesHint: A key with no scopes can still call GET /api/v1/me to verify it works.
apiKeys.copyFailed: Copy failed — select and copy manually

fields.title: Fields & tags
fields.description: Two ways to organize contacts: colour-coded tags for quick grouping, and custom fields for structured data.

custom.title: Custom fields
custom.description: Extra contact fields (e.g. ZIP code, lead source). They appear on every contact and in the “Update Contact Field” automation action.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Colour-coded labels for grouping and filtering contacts.
tags.empty: No tags yet — create your first one below.
tags.deleteDesc: Delete the tag "{name}"? This removes it from all contacts and cannot be undone.

deals.title: Deals & currency
deals.description: The currency used for new deals and for pipeline and dashboard totals.
deals.cardDesc: New deals default to this currency, and pipeline and dashboard totals are shown in it. Existing deals keep the currency they were saved with.
deals.adminOnly: Only account admins can change the default currency.

whatsapp.panelDesc: Connect your Meta WhatsApp Business API. Credentials, webhook, and setup steps all live here.
whatsapp.tokenRequiredInitial: Access Token is required for initial setup
whatsapp.reenterToken: Please re-enter the Access Token to save changes
whatsapp.registerFailed: Saved, but Meta couldn't register the number: {error}
whatsapp.registrationSkipped: Credentials saved and verified. Inbound registration was skipped (no PIN) — see Registration status below.
whatsapp.connectedGeneric: WhatsApp connected. Events will start flowing within a minute.
whatsapp.connectionTestFailed: Connection test failed. Check network and try again.
whatsapp.registrationLive: Number is fully wired — Meta is delivering events.
whatsapp.registrationNotFull: Number is not fully registered. See the checks below for which step failed.
whatsapp.verifyEndpointUnreachable: Could not reach the verification endpoint.
whatsapp.resetConfirm: This will delete the current WhatsApp config so you can re-enter it. Continue?
whatsapp.resetCleared: Configuration cleared. You can now re-enter your credentials.
whatsapp.credsValidDesc: Your access token authenticates with Meta. See Registration status below for whether webhooks are actually wired.
whatsapp.notConnectedDesc: Configure your Meta API credentials below to connect your WhatsApp Business account.
whatsapp.registeredTitle: Registered — Meta will deliver events to wacrm
whatsapp.notRegisteredTitle: Not registered — Meta will not deliver events
whatsapp.registeredDesc: Subscribed since {date}. Click Verify with Meta if events stop arriving.
whatsapp.lastFailedDesc: Last attempt failed with: <err>"{error}"</err>. Enter (or correct) the 2-step PIN below and click Save Configuration to retry.
whatsapp.neverRegisteredDesc: This number was saved before registration tracking existed, or registration was skipped. Enter the 2-step PIN below and click Save Configuration to subscribe it.
whatsapp.credsDesc: Enter your Meta WhatsApp Business API credentials.
whatsapp.tokenHidden: Token is hidden for security. Re-enter it to update configuration.
whatsapp.verifyHint: A custom string you create. Must match the token you set in Meta webhook settings.
whatsapp.pinHint: Needed only to wire inbound messages for a production number. Set it in Meta Business Manager → WhatsApp Accounts → Phone Numbers → Two-step verification, then paste it here so wacrm can subscribe the number — otherwise Meta routes inbound events to whichever app last claimed it (the symptom that hits second numbers under a shared WABA). Meta test numbers have no PIN and are pre-registered — leave this blank for them. Leaving it blank also keeps an existing registration untouched.
whatsapp.webhookDesc: Use this URL as your webhook callback in the Meta App Dashboard.
whatsapp.setupDesc: Follow these steps to connect your WhatsApp Business API.

setup.step1Item4: Fill in app details and create
setup.step2Item1: In your app dashboard, click "Add Product"
setup.step2Item3: Follow the setup wizard to link your business
setup.step3Item4: Generate a Permanent Access Token from Business Settings > System Users
setup.step4Item3: Paste the Webhook Callback URL from above
setup.step4Item4: Enter the same Verify Token you set here

templates.submitEditDone: Edit submitted — Meta typically reviews within 24 hours.
templates.submitNewDone: Submitted to Meta — typical review time is 24 hours. Status updates automatically.
templates.synced: {count, plural, one {Synced # template from Meta} other {Synced # templates from Meta}}
templates.syncTruncated: Synced the first 2000 templates only — your account has more. Sync again to continue, or contact support if this persists.
templates.title: Message templates
templates.description: Create templates and submit them to Meta for approval. Use "Sync from Meta" to pull templates approved elsewhere.
templates.syncTitleAttr: Pull approved templates from your Meta WhatsApp Business Account
templates.emptyHint: Create your first message template to get started.
templates.editTitleAttr: Editing triggers Meta re-review — status flips to PENDING.
templates.resubmitTitleAttr: Edit the template and resubmit to Meta for review.
templates.deleteAriaMeta: Delete template from Meta and locally
templates.editDialogDesc: Save your changes to re-submit to Meta. Status will flip back to PENDING during review.
templates.newDialogDesc: Build a template and submit it to Meta for approval. Once approved, you can use it in broadcasts and the inbox.
templates.authWarning: AUTHENTICATION templates have a fixed body + OTP button shape that needs a different builder. Create them in Meta WhatsApp Manager for now and use Sync from Meta to bring them in.
templates.nameHintEdit: Name is fixed once a template exists on Meta — create a new template to change it.
templates.nameHintNew: Lowercase letters, digits, and underscores only.
templates.langHintEdit: Language is fixed once a template exists on Meta.
templates.langHintNew: Must match the exact code on Meta — en_US and en are distinct.
templates.headerSamplePlaceholder: Sample value for '{{1}}' (required for Meta review)
templates.mediaHintImage: Upload a JPEG/PNG (≤5 MB, ≥800×418 px recommended) or paste a public HTTPS link — we upload it to Meta for review automatically.
templates.mediaHintOther: Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS link. Meta fetches it once during review, so it needs to stay live for ~24 hrs.
templates.headerImageType: Header image must be a JPEG or PNG.
templates.headerImageTooLarge: Image is {size} MB — Meta's limit is 5 MB.
templates.bodyHint: Use '{{1}}', '{{2}}' for variables (must be contiguous starting at '{{1}}').
templates.sampleValuesLabel: Sample values (Meta uses these to review your template)
templates.buttonsHintEmpty: Up to {max} buttons. QUICK_REPLY buttons must come before URL / phone / copy-code buttons.
templates.urlExamplePlaceholder: Example value for '{{1}}' (required when URL has a variable)
templates.deleteDescMeta: "{name}" will be deleted from Meta and from wacrm. Active broadcasts using this template will start failing on their next send. This can't be undone.
templates.deleteDescLocal: "{name}" will be deleted from wacrm. It was never submitted to Meta, so no remote cleanup is needed.
